Context

Why personal branding matters for
established founders

Even the most accomplished builders leave value on the table when their digital presence does not reflect their real world influence. The data is clear.

44%

of a company's market value is directly tied to the CEO's personal brand strength and reputation.

Weber Shandwick, 2024

83%

of CEOs say their personal brand directly impacts business outcomes including investor confidence and deal flow.

PwC CEO Survey, 2024

82%

of people are more likely to trust a company when its senior executives are active and visible on social media.

Entrepreneur, 2024

73%

of decision makers say thought leadership content is more trustworthy than traditional marketing when evaluating a company.

Edelman-LinkedIn, 2024

💡

The Elon Musk Principle

Elon Musk does not just post about Tesla, SpaceX or Grok. He shares his thinking on AI, civilisation, energy and the future. That consistent personal voice has made him one of the most recognised founders on earth. His personal brand amplifies every company he is associated with. The lesson for established founders is not to copy his approach but to understand that a strong personal voice is a multiplier not a distraction. Your story, your beliefs and your perspective are assets that compound over time.

01

The Subject

Meet Mitchell Elegbe

Mitchell Elegbe is the Founder and Group CEO of Interswitch, the company that built the infrastructure Africa's digital economy runs on.

Founded in 2002, Interswitch started as one simple idea. Enable seamless electronic movement of money between banks. What that idea became over 24 years is one of Africa's most consequential technology stories.

Today Interswitch powers digital payments, financial inclusion and transaction processing across the continent. Verve has issued over 100 million cards across Africa. In 2025 the company reported revenue of ₦137 billion. He has been recognised by CNBC Forbes, Ernst and Young, The African Banker and the Silverbird Foundation.

2002

Year Interswitch was founded

100M+

Verve cards issued across Africa

₦137B

Revenue in 2025

"He is not just a fintech founder. He is a builder of Africa's financial rails."

Core Positioning — This Case Study

02

The Observation

His LinkedIn does not
match his legacy

Mitchell Elegbe is one of the most consequential builders in African technology. His work touches millions of lives every day. His LinkedIn page tells almost none of that story.

✕About section written in third person. Reads like a company filing not a personal statement from the man behind the infrastructure.
✕Last repost was Interswitch company content six months ago. Before that an event update six years prior.
✕No hook. No origin story. No human voice. No CTA that invites the right people to connect.
The gap between his real world impact and his digital presence is where this project begins.

03

Research

Competitor Research

I studied 7 founders and executives across Nigerian fintech and global payments companies. The pattern was the same across every profile. They post when something happens. When it does not they go quiet.

Repost of company content

External recognition repost

Product update repost

Industry news repost

Company milestone repost

Global founder — same pattern

What they post

→Reposts of company content
→Award announcements and recognitions
→Product and milestone updates
→Event appearances and external features

What none of them post

✕Original thinking shared consistently
✕Personal stories behind the building journey
✕Opinions on where the industry is going
✕Content that exists independently of a milestone

The personal brand space for founders in this industry is almost entirely unoccupied. That gap is the opportunity this strategy was built to fill.

04

Objectives

The Goals

→Position Mitchell Elegbe as the definitive voice on building lasting technology infrastructure in Africa
→Inspire the next generation of African founders, engineers and builders through consistent original content
→Deepen trust among investors, partners and stakeholders who follow his personal presence
→Create a content system that reflects 24 years of building experience rather than just company updates
→Fill the gap left by a reactive posting pattern that communicates almost none of his real influence

05

Foundation

His Core Belief

Every strategy needs a foundation. For Mitchell Elegbe that foundation is clear and consistent across everything he has built and said publicly.

1

Purpose outlasts profit

Success is not measured in revenue but in lives touched, people empowered and systems built that outlast the individual who created them.

2

Great organisations are built collectively

Interswitch became what it is because a group of people believed in a vision that most institutions had already rejected. Not because of one person.

3

The next generation deserves investment

Not just funding. Not just advice. Real mentorship, opportunity and genuine investment in the people who will build what comes next for Africa.

06

Strategy

His Content Pillars

Three pillars rooted directly in his story, his beliefs and his 24 years of experience. Broad enough to reach a wide audience. Specific enough to attract exactly the right people.

1

Infrastructure Builder

Building what does not exist. Persisting when nobody believes. Creating foundations others build on. This pillar speaks to every founder who has ever been told their vision does not make sense.

2

Africa-Focused Innovator

Building for African context not copying Western models. Local problems require local thinking. African talent can build world class solutions.

3

Purpose-Driven Leader and Mentor

Success defined by impact not profit. Developing the next generation of African builders. Long term thinking over short term wins.

07

Profile

Profile Optimisation

Your profile is your landing page. Every piece of content drives people back to it. If it is not optimised you are leaking the attention you worked to earn.

✕ Current About Section
✓ Rewritten About Section

"Real success is measured in three things. The lives touched. The people empowered. And those who sleep knowing what you built will not fail them."

Opening line · Rewritten About Section

08

System

The Personal Brand System

Building a personal brand that delivers results requires more than posting content. It requires three layers working together.

01

Attention

LinkedIn. Three posts per week. Monday, Wednesday and Friday. Each living inside one of his three content pillars. Not announcements. Not reposts. Original thinking and stories that only he can tell.

02

Nurture

Consistent original thinking posts give his audience a reason to keep coming back. His perspective on African finance and technology. Lessons from 24 years of building. Stories that build the kind of trust a single viral post never can.

03

Monetise

Deeper trust among Interswitch investors and stakeholders. Inbound interest from founders and partners. Speaking invitations from African and global platforms. Legacy positioning as the definitive voice on building technology infrastructure in Africa.

09

Execution

Content Strategy

Every post has a job to do. Some posts reach new people. Some build trust. Some convert attention into action.

Funnel StagePurposeContent Types
Top of FunnelReach and impressions. Get in front of new people.Personal origin stories. Bold observations about Africa's financial ecosystem. Simple ideas with massive impact.
Middle of FunnelTrust and authority. Show depth of experience.Builder's lens on current events. Lessons from resistance. Africa's next chapter. The team behind the infrastructure.
Bottom of FunnelConversion. Move the right people to connect.Mentorship moments. Global exposure and local execution. Selective CTA posts for founders, investors and partners.

Formats

8 Content Formats
to Explore

01

Original Thinking Posts

His perspective on Africa's financial ecosystem. No milestone needed. Just his view on what is shifting and what most people are missing.

02

The Builder's Lens

When something happens in African finance or technology, his actual take from 24 years of building the infrastructure. Not a repost.

03

Lessons from Resistance

The banks that said no. The regulators that stalled. What those moments taught him about persistence and conviction.

04

Africa's Next Chapter

Where he believes the continent is going in the next decade. What needs to be built. Content nobody in his peer group is producing.

05

The Team Behind the Infrastructure

Stories about the people who built Interswitch with him. Collaboration over individual brilliance.

06

Mentorship Moments

Specific lessons he shares with young founders. Not generic advice. Real things he wishes he knew earlier.

07

Simple Ideas, Massive Impact

How one clear idea becomes an ecosystem. Using Interswitch as the case study but connecting it to a universal principle.

08

Global Exposure, Local Execution

What working abroad taught him about building for Nigeria. Content that travels beyond Nigeria and speaks to the African diaspora.

10

Calendar

Two Week Content Calendar

Monday, Wednesday and Friday. Three posts per week. Each one living inside one of the three content pillars with a specific hook and development note.

Week OneMon · Wed · Fri
MON

Original Thinking

"One thing that most people misunderstand about building financial infrastructure in Africa is this…"

Challenge a commonly held assumption. Position him as someone with insider knowledge from 24 years of building.

WED

Personal Story

"In 2002 I watched a system fail the very people it was supposed to serve. That was the day I stopped thinking and started building."

Reconstruct the specific scene. What he saw. What he felt. What he decided in that moment.

FRI

Impact Story

"Before Interswitch, a market woman in Lagos spent every Friday in a bank queue just to have cash for the weekend. Here is what her Fridays look like now."

Research real testimonials of Nigerians whose financial access changed because of Verve or Quickteller. Tell it from their perspective. One person. One specific story. No metrics. No press release language.

Week TwoMon · Wed · Fri
MON

Original Thinking

"24 years ago the things that are now normal in Africa's financial ecosystem were considered impossible. Here is what changed."

Connect the past to the future. Use his unique vantage point to inspire the next generation of builders.

WED

Personal Story

"Interswitch was rejected by every major bank we approached. Here is what that period taught me about knowing when to push and when to walk away."

Reconstruct the early rejections. How many doors were closed. What the resistance sounded like. End with the specific lesson about conviction versus stubbornness and how he knew the difference.

FRI

Product Story

"Interswitch did not start as an ecosystem. It started as one answer to one problem. Here is how one idea became many."

Walk through the evolution from transaction switching to the full ecosystem. Story driven not corporate.

Copywriter & Content Strategist · Social Media Associate at DevCareer

I am a copywriter and content strategist building in public. In 17 days I have produced 13 portfolio pieces including spec copy for Creaibo and Bumpa, four LinkedIn profile audits with verified testimonials and this full personal brand strategy for the CEO of Interswitch.

I currently volunteer as Social Media Associate at DevCareer, a nonprofit empowering African developers and tech professionals. I specialise in copywriting and content strategy for tech and remote brands.
